rationale: The cited paper is used as a core theoretical and empirical precedent, not just background. In the framework section, the citing paper draws on Chandrashekaran et al. (2000) to justify modeling stated judgments with both magnitude and uncertainty, and it explicitly uses the 2000 finding that uncertainty weakens intention-to-behavior translation to motivate its main satisfaction-to-loyalty hypothesis. This citation therefore meaningfully shapes the paper's conceptual setup and hypothesis logic, even though the new study is in a different domain.
relation_description: The citing paper extends Chandrashekaran et al. (2000) by transferring its uncertainty-magnitude and translation logic from turnover intentions to customer satisfaction and loyalty. It builds on that prior evidence to theorize and test how satisfaction uncertainty weakens the conversion of stated satisfaction into loyalty.
